methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Deepnight is a Seed-stage defense and security company founded by Lucas Young and Thomas Li, having raised $5.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a universe of 10 private and 2 public comparables in the Defense & Security sector, including peers such as Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, Saronic, and Quantum-Systems.

The Stage-Progression method is applicable and forms the sole quantitative foundation for this analysis. Using total capital raised ($5.5M) as a proxy baseline and applying a typical Seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x yields an implied valuation of $14M. This method reflects observed progression patterns in early-stage defense technology funding.

The Peer-Median approach is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with sufficient granularity. While 19 peers operate in Defense & Security broadly, filtered subsets yield fewer than the three datapoints required for reliable median-based triangulation. Reliance on broader sector medians would introduce material noise given Deepnight's specific stage and capitalization profile.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $12M (low) to $16M (high), with a median of $14M. This narrow band reflects the single-method foundation. Key uncertainties include product maturity, customer validation, and competitive positioning within a crowded but differentiated peer set. A more robust triangulation would require additional revenue, margin, or customer traction data.
